items.hook: The New York State Register is the official journal of the New York state government that contains information on proposed regulations and rulemaking activities.
items.body: The New York State Register is published weekly by the New York State Department of State's Division of Administrative Rules. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: St Mary the Virgin's Church is a former priory church in the village of Bromfield, Shropshire, England.
items.body: It is an active Anglican parish church in the deanery of Ludlow, the archdeaconry of Ludlow, and the diocese of Hereford. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Fair Salinia was a British Thoroughbred racehorse and broodmare best known for winning the classic Oaks Stakes in 1978.
items.body: In a racing career which lasted from September 1977 until September 1978 she won four of her eight races. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.body: Happiness is a complex and multifaceted mental state that encompasses a range of positive feelings, from contentment to intense joy. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Hunter-gatherers introduced fish to a mountain lake 7000 years ago

items.hook: Colditz is a two-part 2005 television drama miniseries, based on the book Colditz: The Definitive History by Henry Chancellor, and the Channel 4 television series Escape from Colditz.

items.hook: Why a Ukrainian cruise missile is flying with hobby drone hardware
items.body: An open-source flight-control system found in consumer drones has been installed in Ukraine’s latest cruise missile, showing how cheap technology is making military hardware accessible to all
